page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors using the 64-bit ARM architecture with Neoverse-V1 cores. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c7gd.xlarge in the average tier for most general compute and memory operations. It achieves average results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Geekbench single-core and multi-core suites. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 386.53 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 107.62 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU scores fall into the weak category, and cryptographic performance is poor, as indicated by the Passmark CPU encryption test.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a resource-dense profile for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. The inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage makes it highly suitable for applications requiring low-latency scratch space. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and lightweight containerized applications, though it is less optimal for intensive cryptographic tasks or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton processors running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of memory, 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 2 Gbps baseline network interface, all managed via the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard single-core and multi-core CPU tasks, software compilation, and database operations, alongside high cached memory read bandwidth of 386.53 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and cryptographic operations. Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and integrated high-speed local storage, this instance is highly suited for static web serving, software development builds, and local database workloads, offering a cost-efficient ARM-based hardware profile for non-cryptographic, compute-focused tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 237 GB local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 237 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for efficient web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton ARM64 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
Includes 8.0 GB of system memory and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers solid performance for static web serving and database operations
Features a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight databases
